ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-03-2026 Continued To Apr-07-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-26-2025 Continued To Mar-03-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Little for co-administrators Dale Chin and Karen Chin (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Status on Close of Estate.

Mr. Little reported that the estate is awaiting an Inventory and Appraisal for an after-found asset, a 1970 Mercedes convertible. The assigned probate referee advised she could not complete the appraisal and gave no timeline. Mr. Little proposed waiving appraisal and using the vehicle's sale price as its value.

The Court agreed that waiving appraisal would be appropriate and directed counsel to pursue the request as a drop-off ex parte. Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to March 3, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-28-2025 Continued To Nov-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Continued To Oct-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Phillip Campbell, Esq., for Dale Chin, and Karen Chin, Co-Administrators.

At the outset, counsel reports to the court regarding the delays in submitting any filings to close the estate, including closing the related Conservatorship in San Mateo County and waiting for a tax bill from the City and County of San Francisco.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Oct-28-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Peou. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-29-2025 Continued To Jul-29-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Phillip Campbell for Dale Chin and Karen Chin, Co-Administrators (via Zoom).

The court inquired as to the status of the case. Mr. Campbell informed the Court that a recent death in his family and his new responsibilities as caregiver for his mother had significantly impacted his practice and caused delays. Mr. Campbell requested a 60-day continuance. The Court granted a 90-day continuance to allow sufficient time to file the necessary petition.

The matter was continued to July 29, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
